{"id":"auth-sandbox-jux","title":"Create stable tests - Both Flows","description":"Liste alle Flows. Erzeuge stabile Tests mit den devtools:

## Flows im Projekt:
1. **Device Authorization Grant** - Mobile App registriert Device, signiert Challenge mit biometric/PIN-protected Key, erhält JWT Device Token, Keycloak tauscht für OIDC Tokens
2. **SSO Transfer** - Browser-basierte SSO Übergabe: existierende OIDC Session wird auf zweite App übertragen mittels short-lived Transfer Token und Keycloak's PAR + JWT Authorization Grant

Ziel: Stabile, zuverlässige E2E-Tests für beide Flows mit Chrome DevTools erstellen.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"chore","owner":"rene.preissel@etosquare.de","created_at":"2026-03-01T19:59:55Z","created_by":"Rene Preissel","updated_at":"2026-03-01T19:59:55Z"}

Referenz: admin-mock-react/src/services/oidc.ts REDIRECT_URI = 'https://admin.localhost:8443/callback'

{"id":"auth-sandbox-ect","title":"Refactor: cms-admin oidc.ts auf shared @auth-sandbox/utils umstellen","description":"cms-admin-react/src/services/oidc.ts hat eine eigene inline-Fehlerbehandlung bei exchangeCode und refreshTokens (resp.ok Check + resp.text()). admin-mock-react verwendet stattdessen handleApiResponse aus @auth-sandbox/utils, was konsistente Fehlerbehandlung sicherstellt.

Fix:
- Datei: cms-admin-react/src/services/oidc.ts
- Import hinzufügen: import { handleApiResponse } from '@auth-sandbox/utils';
- In exchangeCode(): 'const json = await resp.json()' ersetzen durch 'const json = await handleApiResponse<Record<string, unknown>>(resp)'
- In refreshTokens(): analog ersetzen
- Die manuelle resp.ok Prüfung und throw entfernen (handleApiResponse übernimmt das)

Referenz: admin-mock-react/src/services/oidc.ts Zeilen 101, 124

Falls nicht: in cms-admin-react/package.json als workspace dependency eintragen: '@auth-sandbox/utils': 'workspace:*' und npm install ausführen.

{"id":"auth-sandbox-sb0","title":"Register cms-admin-client in admin-sandbox Keycloak realm","description":"The cms-admin-react SPA needs a Keycloak client 'cms-admin-client' registered in the admin-sandbox realm to enable PKCE login for CMS admins.

Steps in Keycloak Admin UI (https://keycloak.localhost:8443):
1. Log in as admin, switch to realm: admin-sandbox
2. Clients → Create client:
   - Client ID: cms-admin-client
   - Client authentication: OFF (public client, PKCE)
   - Standard Flow: ON, Direct Access Grants: OFF
3. Valid Redirect URIs: https://cms.localhost:8443/cms-admin/*
4. Web Origins: https://cms.localhost:8443
5. Save

Why: Without this client, Keycloak returns 'client not found' when the CMS Admin SPA initiates PKCE auth against admin-sandbox realm.

Test: Navigate to https://cms.localhost:8443/cms-admin/, click Login → redirects to keycloak.localhost:8443/realms/admin-sandbox/... → after login returns to /cms-admin/ with ?code= → SPA exchanges token and shows CMS Admin interface.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"rene.preissel@etosquare.de","created_at":"2026-03-07T07:04:39Z","created_by":"Rene Preissel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T07:04:39Z"}

{"description":"Nach Device-Registrierung und erstem Login soll der User im UI ein Passwort eingeben - nur wenn noch keines gesetzt ist.\\n\\n## Flow\\n1. User registriert Gerät (registerDevice)\\n2. User loggt sich ein (startLogin + verifyLogin) → bekommt Access Token\\n3. Wenn User noch kein Passwort hat:\\n   - UI zeigt Passwort-Eingabe-Dialog\\n   - User gibt neues Passwort ein\\n   - API setzt Passwort in Keycloak\\n4. Wenn User bereits Passwort hat → kein Dialog\\n\\n## Backend: API Endpoint\\n\\n\\n- Token validieren\\n- User-ID aus Token extrahieren\\n- Passwort via Keycloak Admin API setzen (mit Admin-Token)\\n\\n## Frontend: Passwort-Dialog\\n- Nach verifyLogin prüfen ob User Passwort hat (Response-Flag oder separater API-Call)\\n- Wenn kein Passwort → Dialog anzeigen\\n- Nach Passwort-Setzen → weiter wie normal\\n\\n## Technische Details\\n- hasPassword() Methode in KeycloakAdminClient → behalten für Check\\n- UPDATE_PASSWORD Required Action → kann entfallen (UI zeigt Dialog)\\n\\n## Tests\\n- E2E-Test für den complete Flow\\n- Unit-Tests für API-Endpoint","status":"open","title":"Passwort-Setzen nach erstem Login (UI + API)"}
old_value: {"id":"auth-sandbox-7sl","title":"Passwort-Setzen Endpoint für User nach Device-Registrierung","description":"Nach Device-Registrierung soll der User per API sein Passwort setzen können.\\n\\n## Was zu tun ist\\n\\nNeuer Endpoint:\\n\\n\\n1. Endpoint in DeviceService oder neuem Controller anlegen\\n2. User-Access-Token validieren (OidcTokenValidationService)\\n3. User-ID aus Token extrahieren\\n4. Passwort via Keycloak Admin API setzen (mit Admin-Token)\\n\\n## Hintergrund\\n- User hat nach Device-Registrierung kein Passwort\\n- UPDATE_PASSWORD Required Action wird gesetzt\\n- Bisher muss User über Keycloak Web UI gehen\\n- Ziel: User kann direkt per API Passwort setzen mit seinem Access Token","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"rene.preissel@etosquare.de","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:27:28Z","created_by":"Rene Preissel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T16:27:28Z"}

User registriert Gerät (registerDevice)\\n2. User loggt sich ein (startLogin + verifyLogin) → bekommt Access Token\\n3. {"notes":"Verified: 
- Caddyfile uses 'tls /etc/caddy/certs/keycloak.localhost+7.pem /etc/caddy/certs/keycloak.localhost+7-key.pem' for ALL vhosts
- caddy_data/certs/keycloak.localhost+7.pem issuer = mkcert CA
- mkcert CA root at: /Users/rene/Library/Application Support/mkcert/rootCA.pem
- node --input-type=module with mkcert CA: Status 200 ✓
- node --input-type=module with caddy internal CA: Error 'unable to verify the first certificate'

Fix location: e2e/global-setup.ts, section 2 (lines starting with 'Extract Caddy root CA')

The fix should:
1. Try mkcert -CAROOT to get the CA root dir  
2. Read rootCA.pem from that dir
3. Copy it to certs/caddy-root.crt (so existing E2E_CA_CERT_PATH logic still works)
4. Remove the faulty 'docker exec caddy cat /data/caddy/pki/...' logic

Fallback: if mkcert is not installed, check if ./caddy_data/certs/ cert issuer matches any known CA, or throw a helpful error."}
old_value:
{"id":"auth-sandbox-zag","title":"Fix e2e global-setup: use mkcert CA instead of Caddy internal CA","description":"The e2e tests hang on waitForHttp() because global-setup.ts extracts Caddy's internal ECC root CA (/data/caddy/pki/authorities/local/root.crt) and writes it to certs/caddy-root.crt. However, the Caddyfile does NOT use 'tls internal' — it serves mkcert certificates from ./caddy_data/certs/keycloak.localhost+7.pem which were issued by the local mkcert CA. Node's https module fails with 'unable to verify the first certificate' because the stored CA cert does not match the certificate issuer.

Root cause: global-setup.ts step 2 overwrites certs/caddy-root.crt with Caddy's internal CA cert, but the actual TLS termination uses mkcert certs.

Fix: Replace step 2 in e2e/global-setup.ts with logic that uses the mkcert rootCA.pem from the mkcert CAROOT directory (process output of 'mkcert -CAROOT', or hardcoded fallback path). The mkcert CA root is at ~/Library/Application Support/mkcert/rootCA.pem on macOS.

E2E_CA_CERT_PATH should point to the mkcert rootCA.pem (or a copy of it) so that all https requests in the test suite trust the mkcert-issued server certificates.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rene.preissel@etosquare.de","created_at":"2026-03-07T19:21:37Z","created_by":"Rene Preissel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T19:21:37Z"}

new_value:
{"notes":"Fixed multiple issues in e2e test setup:

1. CA Certificate Fix (root cause):
   - Changed global-setup.ts to use mkcert root CA instead of Caddy's internal CA
   - mkcert CA is at ~/Library/Application Support/mkcert/rootCA.pem or via 'mkcert -CAROOT'
   - This fixes 'unable to verify the first certificate' errors

2. Keycloak URL Fix:
   - Override KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_TOKEN_ENDPOINT to use external URL (https://keycloak.localhost:8443)
   - Tests run on host, not in container, so need external URLs
   - Added override in global-setup.ts

3. Client Fix:
   - Changed default keycloakTokenUrl to https://keycloak.localhost:8443 (was http://keycloak:8080)
   - Replaced fetch() with https.request in getAdminToken() to use custom CA agent
   - This ensures TLS verification works with mkcert certificates

Results:
- Tests now pass global-setup (auth-service and Keycloak health checks pass)
- Admin API calls now get through (401 auth works, but some tests fail on 'Unknown userId')
- Remaining failures are business logic issues, not infrastructure issues"}
old_value:
{"id":"auth-sandbox-zag","title":"Fix e2e global-setup: use mkcert CA instead of Caddy internal CA","description":"The e2e tests hang on waitForHttp() because global-setup.ts extracts Caddy's internal ECC root CA (/data/caddy/pki/authorities/local/root.crt) and writes it to certs/caddy-root.crt. However, the Caddyfile does NOT use 'tls internal' — it serves mkcert certificates from ./caddy_data/certs/keycloak.localhost+7.pem which were issued by the local mkcert CA. Node's https module fails with 'unable to verify the first certificate' because the stored CA cert does not match the certificate issuer.

Root cause: global-setup.ts step 2 overwrites certs/caddy-root.crt with Caddy's internal CA cert, but the actual TLS termination uses mkcert certs.

Fix: Replace step 2 in e2e/global-setup.ts with logic that uses the mkcert rootCA.pem from the mkcert CAROOT directory (process output of 'mkcert -CAROOT', or hardcoded fallback path). The mkcert CA root is at ~/Library/Application Support/mkcert/rootCA.pem on macOS.

E2E_CA_CERT_PATH should point to the mkcert rootCA.pem (or a copy of it) so that all https requests in the test suite trust the mkcert-issued server certificates.","notes":"Verified: 
- Caddyfile uses 'tls /etc/caddy/certs/keycloak.localhost+7.pem /etc/caddy/certs/keycloak.localhost+7-key.pem' for ALL vhosts
- caddy_data/certs/keycloak.localhost+7.pem issuer = mkcert CA
- mkcert CA root at: /Users/rene/Library/Application Support/mkcert/rootCA.pem
- node --input-type=module with mkcert CA: Status 200 ✓
- node --input-type=module with caddy internal CA: Error 'unable to verify the first certificate'

Fix location: e2e/global-setup.ts, section 2 (lines starting with 'Extract Caddy root CA')

The fix should:
1. Try mkcert -CAROOT to get the CA root dir  
2. Read rootCA.pem from that dir
3. Copy it to certs/caddy-root.crt (so existing E2E_CA_CERT_PATH logic still works)
4. Remove the faulty 'docker exec caddy cat /data/caddy/pki/...' logic

Fallback: if mkcert is not installed, check if ./caddy_data/certs/ cert issuer matches any known CA, or throw a helpful error.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rene.preissel@etosquare.de","created_at":"2026-03-07T19:21:37Z","created_by":"Rene Preissel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T19:30:39Z"}

{"id":"auth-sandbox-9f4","title":"SecurityConfig: /api/v1/users/me/** als permitAll freigeben","description":"## Problem

Der Endpoint `GET /api/v1/users/me/password-status` und `POST /api/v1/users/me/password` fallen in der `SecurityConfig` unter `.anyRequest().authenticated()` — Spring Security erwartet deshalb ein **JWT Bearer Token, das vom konfigurierten OAuth2 Resource Server (JWK Set URI des admin-sandbox Realms) validiert wird**.

Die App schickt jedoch das Access Token des `auth-sandbox`-Realms, das gegen den admin-sandbox JWKS-Endpoint nicht validiert werden kann → HTTP 401.

Der `UserController` macht Token-Validierung eigenständig via Keycloak-Introspection — Spring Securitys JWT-Validierung ist dort nicht gewünscht.

## Was zu tun ist

Datei: `auth-service/src/main/java/dev/authsandbox/authservice/config/SecurityConfig.java`

In der `filterChain`-Methode den Block `.authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth...)` um `/api/v1/users/me/**` in der `permitAll()`-Liste erweitern:

**Vorher:**
```java
.requestMatchers(
    "/api/v1/devices/**",
    "/api/v1/auth/**",
    "/api/v1/transfer/**",
    "/p/**",
    "/cms/**",
    "/actuator/health",
    "/actuator/info"
).permitAll()
```

**Nachher:**
```java
.requestMatchers(
    "/api/v1/devices/**",
    "/api/v1/auth/**",
    "/api/v1/transfer/**",
    "/api/v1/users/me/**",
    "/p/**",
    "/cms/**",
    "/actuator/health",
    "/actuator/info"
).permitAll()
```

## Warum permitAll?

Der UserController macht Token-Validierung selbst via Keycloak-Introspection (identisch zu DeviceController, AuthController). Der Spring OAuth2 Resource Server ist für Admin-Endpoints (/api/v1/admin/**) konfiguriert, nicht für User-Endpoints mit eigenem Token-Handling.

## Tests

```bash
cd auth-service && ./gradlew test
```

Alle bestehenden Tests müssen weiterhin grün sein.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rene.preissel@etosquare.de","created_at":"2026-03-07T22:20:19Z","created_by":"Rene Preissel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T22:20:19Z"}

new_value: Fixed: added /api/v1/users/me/** to permitAll list
